AI-300|T2|Q1.rationale: Standardizing ML asset management starts with a shared Azure Machine Learning workspace - it is the top-level container that centrally manages data, models, environments and compute. Registries (A) are for cross-workspace sharing and come later.

AI-300|T2|Q2.rationale: Managed compute targets with autoscaling isolate workloads while staying cost-aware: nodes scale up on demand and back to zero when idle, avoiding the contention of a shared cluster (A) and the waste of a fixed-size (B) or always-dedicated (C) cluster.

AI-300|T2|Q4.rationale: Azure Machine Learning compute clusters support low-priority (spot) VMs at a discount. ACI and AKS do not offer the low-priority/spot pricing tier, and local deployment has no Azure discount.

AI-300|T2|Q5.rationale: For a safe, gradual rollout you split traffic between the existing and new deployments behind one online endpoint (blue/green / canary). Replacing or routing 100% (C, D) removes the staged rollback ability; batch (A) is not for live traffic.

AI-300|T2|Q6.rationale: Azure OpenAI fine-tuning and most generative training datasets require JSONL (one JSON object per line). CSV/TSV/plain JSON are not the expected format.

AI-300|T2|Q7.rationale: UTF-8 is the required/standard encoding for Azure AI training and JSONL data files.

AI-300|T2|Q8.rationale: Before fine-tuning you must prepare and upload the training data as a JSONL file of prompt-completion (or messages) pairs. The other steps are not the prerequisite first action.

AI-300|T2|Q9.rationale: To evaluate prompt variants with consistent inputs and no live traffic, first generate synthetic interaction/test data to use as a controlled evaluation dataset.

AI-300|T2|Q11.rationale: To make an asset reusable and governable across workspaces/projects, publish it to an Azure Machine Learning registry. Git sync, pipeline components and environments do not provide the cross-workspace asset sharing a registry does.

AI-300|T2|Q12.rationale: Private endpoints restrict access to Azure ML / dependent resources over a private IP and remove public exposure - the standard way to meet 'no public network access'. NSGs/firewall/service endpoints are weaker, subnet-level controls.

AI-300|T2|Q13.rationale: Registering the Model asset makes the trained artifact versioned, reusable and governed across projects. Components, environments and pipelines serve other purposes.

AI-300|T2|Q16.rationale: No. 'python train.py --training_data training_data' runs the wrong script (train.py vs script.py) and passes the literal string 'training_data' instead of binding the job input. It does not pass the dataset path.

AI-300|T2|Q17.rationale: No. The script reads an argument named 'training_data', but the command supplies '--trainingdata' (missing underscore), so the argument name does not match. Community also voted B.

AI-300|T2|Q18.rationale: No. 'python script.py dataset1.csv' hard-codes the file as a positional value and does not bind it to the job input '${{inputs.training_data}}', so it does not pass the parameter correctly for a command job.

AI-300|T3|Q1.rationale: MLflow experiment tracking is the built-in way to record and compare metrics/params/artifacts across Azure ML training runs. Job logs, App Insights and Monitor alerts are not experiment-comparison tools.

AI-300|T3|Q3.rationale: fairlearn.postprocessing.ThresholdOptimizer adjusts decision thresholds on an already-trained model to satisfy fairness constraints without retraining - the typical post-hoc mitigation.

AI-300|T3|Q9.rationale: When a new deployment is unhealthy, the first mitigation is to roll traffic back to the previous known-good deployment to restore service, then investigate.

AI-300|T3|Q13.rationale: Archive the model version to retire it from active use while preserving lineage/auditability. Deleting or unregistering destroys history.

AI-300|T3|Q14.rationale: Traffic splitting between deployments on the online endpoint enables safe staged rollout/comparison of a new model version.

AI-300|T4|Q3.rationale: Configure a managed virtual network for the Foundry resource (A) and disable public network access (D) to lock it down. API keys (B) violate least-privilege/no-secrets; a separate subscription (C) and disabling all inbound (E) are not the required network-isolation steps. Matches community AD.

AI-300|T4|Q7.rationale: Store prompts as versioned files in Git for change history, review and rollback - prompt assets are code-like and benefit from source control.

AI-300|T4|Q8.rationale: Data Zone Standard gives low-latency, real-time (non-batch) interactive throughput within a data zone for many applications. Batch types add latency; Developer is for limited dev use.

AI-300|T4|Q9.rationale: Use Entra ID RBAC with least privilege: assign Azure AI Developer to developers (C) and an appropriate admin role where needed (A). Disabling Entra auth (B) and sharing one API key (D) violate the security requirements.

AI-300|T4|Q10.rationale: gpt-4o is the multimodal Azure OpenAI model that accepts both text and images and supports fine-tuning. davinci-002, gpt-35-turbo and gpt-4 (base) do not fit the text+image fine-tune requirement.

AI-300|T4|Q11.rationale: Yes. The Evaluation experience with prompt variants captures inputs, outputs, token usage and latency for comparison, meeting the goal. Community voted A.

AI-300|T4|Q12.rationale: Yes. Enabling tracing via the prompt flow SDK before runs captures inputs, outputs, token usage and latencies - a valid solution to the same goal.

AI-300|T4|Q13.rationale: Yes. Turning on Tracing for the project's prompt flow in Foundry and running tests produces the required trace data (inputs/outputs/tokens/latency). NOTE: community voted B for this variant - verify, as wording differences in this Yes/No series matter.

AI-300|T5|Q3.rationale: Groundedness measures whether a generated answer is supported by the provided source/context - the evaluator for RAG factual grounding. Coherence/fluency/similarity assess other qualities.

AI-300|T5|Q4.rationale: RelevanceEvaluator scores how relevant the response is to the question/context. Use it when relevance is the target metric.

AI-300|T5|Q6.rationale: Implement an evaluation step in CI (GitHub Actions) so prompts/models are automatically evaluated before deployment - the repeatable, automated gate. Manual review and latency monitoring are not automated quality gates.

AI-300|T5|Q7.rationale: For a RAG solution, configure the Groundedness evaluator first to ensure answers are supported by retrieved content. Protected material/indirect attacks/content safety address different risks.

AI-300|T6|Q1.rationale: Choose a model fit for purpose: conversational-reasoning model for an interactive assistant (A), multimodal when combining text+image (D), and one with enterprise governance for regulated data (E). 'Largest' (B) and 'smallest' (C) as blanket rules are wrong. Matches community ADE.

AI-300|T6|Q7.rationale: To improve RAG retrieval quality, tune chunk size/overlap to the content (A) and add an optimized re-ranker (B). Raising token limits (C) and changing embedding length (D) do not directly fix retrieval relevance.

AI-103|T1|Q2.rationale: PII Detection prevents the agent from revealing customer information even if a document with customer data is added by mistake. Prompt shields target injection; content filters target harmful categories. Community voted C.

AI-103|T1|Q3.rationale: To let the agent use the product sheets, add a connection to the Azure AI Search resource in the project so the agent can retrieve from the index.

AI-103|T1|Q9.rationale: When multiple agents must collaborate on one task, use a multi-agent group chat session so agents coordinate within the orchestration rather than ad-hoc app code.

AI-103|T1|Q12.rationale: When a custom speech model expires, recognition requests fall back to the most recent base model for the same locale; they do not error out or auto-delete the model.

AI-103|T1|Q13.rationale: To call Azure OpenAI models, assign Cognitive Services OpenAI User (least-privilege data-plane access for inference). Contributor is over-privileged; the others do not grant OpenAI inference.

AI-103|T1|Q14.rationale: To require the agent to use an MCP tool, set tool_choice to the mcp type. 'auto' leaves it optional; 'required' is not the MCP-specific binding; knowledge_base is not the MCP tool type.

AI-103|T1|Q16.rationale: Because API keys are not allowed and Entra auth is required, configure a Bearer token security scheme in the OpenAPI spec so the tool authenticates with an Entra token.

AI-103|T1|Q17.rationale: A model cascade routes easy requests to a smaller/cheaper model and escalates only hard ones to the capable model, balancing cost and quality. Routing all to one model wastes cost or quality.

AI-103|T1|Q19.rationale: To judge output quality/correctness, use evaluation metrics. Latency, run success rate and token usage are operational, not quality, signals.

AI-103|T1|Q21.rationale: Connect the tool to the existing Connection1 so it uses the configured, secured connection rather than manual API keys or caller identity passthrough.

AI-103|T1|Q22.rationale: Tracing gives the per-step execution detail needed to diagnose where/why an agent run behaves unexpectedly. Token usage/monitoring/safety metrics are aggregate signals.

AI-103|T1|Q23.rationale: No. A prompt shield for user prompts guards text prompt injection, not hidden instructions embedded inside uploaded images. It does not mitigate the image-based risk.

AI-103|T1|Q24.rationale: Yes. Image moderation blocks unsafe images before processing, which directly mitigates the unsafe-image upload risk in this scenario.

AI-103|T1|Q25.rationale: No. Prompt shields for documents target injected instructions in grounding documents/text, not malicious instructions hidden in uploaded images.

AI-103|T1|Q26.rationale: No. Protected material detection flags copyrighted content, not unsafe images or hidden image-borne instructions, so it does not meet the goal.

AI-103|T1|Q27.rationale: Use a groundedness evaluator to detect responses not supported by retrieved content (hallucinations). A RAG evaluator is broader; guardrails/fine-tuning are not the measurement asked for.

AI-103|T1|Q28.rationale: Modify the system message instructions to constrain behavior/scope - the most direct, reliable control. Few-shot, top-p and temperature do not enforce scope as deterministically.

AI-103|T1|Q29.rationale: A multimodal model is needed to analyze invoices by combining visual layout and textual content. An SLM/LLM (text-only) or key-phrase model cannot evaluate the visual layout.

AI-103|T1|Q31.rationale: Configure one Azure AI Search connection in Project1 and reference it from each application - centralized, governed, least-privilege. Per-app custom HTTP/direct calls duplicate config and weaken governance.

AI-103|T1|Q34.rationale: Persist conversation context by storing the conversation/thread ID and supplying it on subsequent requests, so the service retains state across turns.

AI-103|T1|Q36.rationale: Agentic RAG lets the agent plan and run multiple targeted retrievals/reasoning steps for complex queries, improving completeness over a single classic RAG pass.

AI-103|T1|Q38.rationale: Switch to Retrieval Augmented Generation so responses are grounded in source content, improving completeness/accuracy. Token/model tweaks do not add the missing grounded content.

AI-103|T1|Q39.rationale: For throttling/429 errors, implement a retry policy with exponential backoff and jitter. New threads, fewer tools or smaller files do not address rate limiting.

AI-103|T1|Q41.rationale: No. Increasing max_tokens only raises the output length cap; it does not make the model include required clauses that it is already omitting.

AI-103|T1|Q42.rationale: Yes. A reflection pass that regenerates the response when required clauses are missing directly improves completeness.

AI-103|T1|Q43.rationale: No. Increasing temperature adds randomness; it does not improve completeness and may worsen omission of required clauses.

AI-103|T1|Q44.rationale: Yes. An evaluation flow that scores completeness and blocks responses below a threshold enforces complete responses, meeting the goal.

AI-103|T1|Q45.rationale: Background removal that preserves the subject is best done with mask inpainting: supply the image plus a mask marking the region to change. Full image_to_image regeneration risks altering the subject.

AI-103|T1|Q46.rationale: To remove a specific logo while keeping the rest, apply mask-based inpainting to the logo region. Prompt/seed/guidance changes regenerate the whole image unpredictably.

AI-103|T1|Q47.rationale: prebuilt-layout extracts tables, structure and layout from documents with varied layouts - needed for invoices. prebuilt-read is OCR-only; the others are not layout extractors.

AI-103|T1|Q48.rationale: Set input_fidelity to high to preserve fine detail/text from the input image during image editing/generation.

AI-103|T1|Q50.rationale: To screen text embedded in images, run OCR (Azure Vision) and apply keyword scanning over the OCR output. Prompt shields/blocklists/image moderation do not scan extracted in-image text the same way.

AI-103|T1|Q51.rationale: Prompt shields for documents protect against injected instructions hidden in ingested documents/grounding content.

AI-103|T1|Q53.rationale: Azure Content Understanding extracts structured fields from mixed-layout documents (tables, logos, varied layouts) - suited to invoice processing.

AI-103|T1|Q56.rationale: Azure Content Understanding handles multi-format/multimodal extraction for varied invoice layouts. Document Intelligence/Image Analysis/chat completions are narrower.

AI-103|T1|Q58.rationale: For large volumes of recorded audio files, run a batch transcription job - the scalable, asynchronous option. Real-time is for live streams.

AI-103|T1|Q59.rationale: For a live voice agent, use real-time speech to text for incoming audio and text to speech for responses - the standard low-latency conversational pattern.

AI-103|T1|Q60.rationale: Split mixed-language segments into single-language segments and translate each separately so Translator handles each correctly. Treating mixed text as one document/source language degrades quality.

AI-103|T1|Q62.rationale: Azure AI Search provides the index/retrieval layer for grounding the agent on the documents.

AI-103|T1|Q63.rationale: Configure content=markdown so the analyzer returns structured markdown (preserving tables/headings) for downstream processing.

AI-103|T1|Q64.rationale: Use a custom Azure Content Understanding analyzer that defines the required invoice fields as the extraction schema - tailored field extraction across varied layouts. Prebuilt-layout/documentSearch are generic.

AI-103|T1|Q65.rationale: When responses are incomplete/ungrounded, review groundedness evaluation metrics to see whether outputs are supported by retrieved content. Indexer status/latency/drift address other failure modes.

AZ-400|T1|Q1.rationale: Cycle time measures elapsed time from when a work item becomes active to completion. Lead time is creation-to-done; burn-up/down track scope/remaining work.

AZ-400|T1|Q2.rationale: Creation-to-completion elapsed time is Lead time, not Burnup, so the underlined segment is inaccurate.

AZ-400|T1|Q3.rationale: License procurement (buying licenses) cannot be automated; group membership, license assignment and entitlements can.

AZ-400|T1|Q4.rationale: Static code analysis runs in CI against source without executing it. Pen/dynamic testing need a running app; threat modeling is design-phase.

AZ-400|T1|Q5.rationale: TFS Database Import Service migrates a full collection to Azure DevOps Services preserving history with minimal effort.

AZ-400|T1|Q7.rationale: The CMMI process supports requirements, change requests, risks and reviews as work item types.

AZ-400|T1|Q8.rationale: No. ApplyOnly applies config once and does not correct drift, so servers won't stay configured.

AZ-400|T1|Q9.rationale: No. ApplyAndMonitor reports drift but does not auto-correct it.

AZ-400|T1|Q10.rationale: Yes. ApplyAndAutoCorrect reapplies configuration to fix drift, keeping servers correctly configured.

AZ-400|T1|Q11.rationale: Building an IIS web app that runs Docker needs a Hosted Windows Container agent, so 'Default' is inaccurate.

AZ-400|T1|Q12.rationale: Conditional Access in Azure AD restricts access to devices on the corporate network (location-based).

AZ-400|T1|Q13.rationale: Git in Azure Repos is the managed, server-hosted Git option within Azure DevOps for this scenario.

AZ-400|T1|Q14.rationale: JIRA is work-item tracking, replaced by Azure Boards, so 'build pipelines' is inaccurate.

AZ-400|T1|Q15.rationale: npm install (with --production) restricts scanning to production dependencies.

AZ-400|T1|Q16.rationale: Red/Black (blue-green) keeps deploy time low and allows the fastest rollback by switching back to the previous environment.

AZ-400|T1|Q17.rationale: A release gate evaluates Azure Monitor performance criteria and blocks promotion when baselines aren't met.

AZ-400|T1|Q18.rationale: Deploying to Azure VMs uses a deployment group, so 'universal group' is inaccurate.

AZ-400|T1|Q20.rationale: Feed views (e.g. @prerelease/@release) control which package quality levels are exposed from a single feed.

AZ-400|T1|Q21.rationale: WhiteSource detects open-source libraries (and license/vuln issues) added to the codebase.

AZ-400|T1|Q22.rationale: Create a build task using WhiteSource Bolt to scan open-source libraries for security weaknesses.

AZ-400|T1|Q23.rationale: Black Duck does perform open-source license compliance scanning, so no adjustment is required.

AZ-400|T1|Q24.rationale: PowerShell DSC extension (B) enforces uniform VM configuration; Azure Pipeline deployment groups (C) manage the VMs with low effort. NOTE: some sources list A+B - verify.

AZ-400|T1|Q25.rationale: Azure Security Center's Compute & Apps blade gives security recommendations for App Service web apps and functions.

AZ-400|T1|Q26.rationale: Average page load times come from Azure Application Insights, not Event Hubs.

AZ-400|T1|Q27.rationale: No. CQL is not the query language for Application Insights / Log Analytics (that's KQL).

AZ-400|T1|Q28.rationale: No. Transact-SQL is not used for ad-hoc queries over Application Insights monitoring data (KQL is).

AZ-400|T1|Q29.rationale: Yes. Azure Log Analytics (KQL) is the correct way to run ad-hoc queries against the monitoring data.

AZ-400|T1|Q31.rationale: The Microsoft Test & Feedback (App Center / Visual Studio) tooling collects crash reports, distributes beta builds and gathers feedback.

AZ-400|T2|Q3.rationale: An Azure Monitor alert with a dynamic threshold detects abnormal usage; an Automation runbook raises the logging level with minimal overhead.

AZ-400|T2|Q5.rationale: An Application Insights availability (URL ping) test runs every 5 min from regions and alerts on outages with minimal dev time.

AZ-400|T2|Q6.rationale: Application Insights reports average page load times for the App Service front end.

AZ-400|T2|Q8.rationale: The IT Service Management Connector (ITSM) creates ServiceNow tickets from Azure alerts/logs.

AZ-400|T2|Q10.rationale: Kusto Query Language (KQL) is used for ad-hoc queries over Application Insights / Log Analytics data.

AZ-400|T2|Q11.rationale: Application Insights (E) captures exceptions; a Logic App (A) posts the daily summary to Microsoft Teams.

AZ-400|T2|Q13.rationale: Visual Studio App Center integration collects crash reports, distributes beta builds and gathers user feedback.

AZ-400|T2|Q14.rationale: Upload a new custom script to sa1 (C), update the Custom Script extension (D), and update a VM's config (E) to canary the new version on part of VMSS1.

AZ-400|T2|Q20.rationale: Smart Detection automatically alerts on sudden rises in failures/performance issues in Application Insights.

AZ-400|T2|Q22.rationale: Add the Azure Boards app to the GitHub repository first to surface commit status on Boards.

AZ-400|T2|Q23.rationale: Run '@azure pipelines subscriptions' in Teams to manage subscriptions and filter to failed builds only.

AZ-400|T2|Q24.rationale: Configure a service hook subscription in Azure DevOps to send work-item update notifications to Teams. NOTE: community voted B (Teams connector) - both are defensible; verify.

AZ-400|T2|Q25.rationale: Activity-log alert rules fire on operations; a failed delete attempt matches the rule's signal.

AZ-400|T2|Q26.rationale: Send diagnostics to Log Analytics and alert on the connection metric with minimal effort.

AZ-400|T2|Q28.rationale: Service Map requires the Dependency agent (alongside the Log Analytics agent).

AZ-400|T2|Q30.rationale: Import the Firebase module into UIApplicationDelegate to capture crash-free users, custom events and breadcrumbs.

AZ-400|T2|Q31.rationale: The Dependency Tracker extension manages producers/consumers across projects with an overview.

AZ-400|T2|Q34.rationale: OpenTelemetry and OpenCensus integrate directly with Application Insights.

AZ-400|T2|Q35.rationale: Fixed-rate sampling for a .NET Core app is configured in Startup.cs (ApplicationInsights.config is for .NET Framework).

AZ-400|T2|Q38.rationale: Set the Application Insights connection string/instrumentation key in appsettings.json.

AZ-400|T2|Q42.rationale: The Microsoft Monitoring/Enterprise Cloud Monitoring extension needs the workspace ID (D) and the workspace key/secret (A).

AZ-400|T2|Q43.rationale: The Events pane shows how often a specific page/event is accessed.

AZ-400|T2|Q46.rationale: App Service diagnostics 'Availability and Performance' identifies when the app was offline.

AZ-400|T2|Q47.rationale: Prometheus metrics are queried with PromQL.

AZ-400|T3|Q1.rationale: Create a Service Health alert (C) for planned maintenance and an action group (D) configured with an SMS action.

AZ-400|T3|Q2.rationale: Yes. Azure Monitor autoscale settings can email on scale in/out via the autoscale notifications.

AZ-400|T3|Q3.rationale: Smart Detection needs ~24 hours to learn the app's normal behavior before it can alert.

AZ-400|T3|Q5.rationale: Use a Request trigger so the availability alert webhook can invoke the logic app over HTTP.

AZ-400|T3|Q6.rationale: A liveness probe restarts the container when the app stops responding.

AZ-400|T3|Q7.rationale: Azure Log Analytics centrally stores app+DB metrics, supports ad-hoc KQL baselines and alerting.

AZ-400|T3|Q8.rationale: Smart Detection in Application Insights flags abnormal rises in failed requests with minimal effort.

AZ-400|T3|Q10.rationale: Azure dashboards give a consolidated, RBAC-controlled view with KQL visuals, markdown tiles and live data.

AZ-400|T3|Q11.rationale: Selenium is the standard framework for automated web UI testing.

AZ-400|T3|Q12.rationale: Add Application Insights telemetry (C) and enable adaptive sampling (B) to capture a baseline while minimizing storage cost.

AZ-400|T3|Q13.rationale: The Application Health extension reports per-instance health for VMSS rolling upgrades with minimal effort (works with mutual-auth HTTPS).

AZ-400|T3|Q15.rationale: Manually mark the test as flaky (D) and exclude flaky tests from the pass percentage (B) so the build won't fail on it.

AZ-400|T3|Q16.rationale: A release gate enforces the Azure Monitor performance baseline before production deployment.

AZ-400|T3|Q17.rationale: No. A Subscription Health scan does not lock project configuration from drifting over time.

AZ-400|T3|Q18.rationale: Security Center provides vulnerability assessments for the Ubuntu VMs (C) and the container images in ACR (E).

AZ-400|T3|Q19.rationale: Dependabot already opens the PR with the update; you approve the pull request to apply it.

AZ-400|T3|Q20.rationale: No. Adding a code coverage step does not prevent project configuration from changing.

AZ-400|T3|Q21.rationale: No. Implementing CI does not lock project configuration against drift.

AZ-400|T3|Q22.rationale: Yes. Continuous Assurance (AzSK) continuously checks and enforces the secure configuration over time.

AZ-400|T3|Q23.rationale: Azure App Configuration supports feature flags, change history, hierarchical keys, RBAC and shared key/value pairs.

AZ-400|T3|Q24.rationale: A readiness probe holds traffic until DB1 finishes loading and can handle requests.

AZ-400|T3|Q25.rationale: Deploy the Log Analytics agent (B) plus the Dependency agent (D) to collect detailed guest process data.

AZ-400|T3|Q27.rationale: No. Azure DevOps project Notifications are for work-item/build events, not VMSS scale events.

AZ-400|T3|Q28.rationale: No. Azure DevOps Service hooks do not observe VMSS autoscale events.

AZ-400|T3|Q29.rationale: Yes. An Azure Monitor action group (with email) combined with the autoscale notification generates the email alert.

AZ-400|T4|Q2.rationale: Conditional Access enforces MFA from untrusted/locations for Azure apps.

AZ-400|T4|Q3.rationale: A self-hosted agent registers using a personal access token (PAT).

AZ-400|T4|Q4.rationale: First create an app registration in Azure AD to enable Azure AD authentication for the app.

AZ-400|T4|Q5.rationale: Personal access tokens support Git auth and minimize repeated credential prompts.

AZ-400|T4|Q6.rationale: Azure Security Center's Compute & Apps provides security recommendations for web apps and functions.

AZ-400|T4|Q7.rationale: Add an Azure Key Vault task so the pipeline fetches secrets at runtime instead of storing credentials.

AZ-400|T4|Q9.rationale: The app must Get and List secrets to read configuration; that is the minimal needed set here.

AZ-400|T4|Q12.rationale: Grant the user 'Bypass policies when completing pull requests' on the branch Security settings (least privilege).

AZ-400|T4|Q13.rationale: Reference secrets from Azure Key Vault so the deploying user never sees the credentials/connection strings.

AZ-400|T4|Q18.rationale: Deploy a VM with a system-assigned managed identity (E), grant it access via a Key Vault access policy (C), and modify the app to read from the vault (B).

AZ-400|T4|Q20.rationale: Create a variable group linked to the key vault to reference its secrets in the pipeline.

AZ-400|T4|Q22.rationale: The policy enforces 'secure transfer required', ensuring traffic to new storage accounts is encrypted (HTTPS).

AZ-400|T4|Q23.rationale: A variable group linked to vault1 lets all pipelines reference the secrets without storing values.

AZ-400|T4|Q27.rationale: Run npm install --production (A) and move dev-only packages into devDependencies (C) so only prod deps are scanned.

AZ-400|T4|Q28.rationale: No. CI alone does not identify licensing violations / prohibited libraries.

AZ-400|T4|Q29.rationale: No. Pre-deployment gates do not detect licensing/prohibited-library issues.

AZ-400|T4|Q30.rationale: Yes. Automated security testing (dependency/license scanning) detects licensing violations and prohibited libraries.

AZ-400|T4|Q31.rationale: No. Continuous deployment does not identify licensing/prohibited-library issues.

AZ-400|T4|Q34.rationale: First register GitHub as an application in Azure AD to use Azure AD authentication.

AZ-400|T4|Q35.rationale: A deployment gate can evaluate Azure Policy compliance and block non-compliant releases.

AZ-400|T4|Q37.rationale: WhiteSource Bolt scans open-source libraries for license compliance.

AZ-400|T4|Q38.rationale: SonarQube identifies dependencies with known security issues that an update can resolve.

AZ-400|T4|Q39.rationale: Contributor access lets developers unlist/deprecate packages (least privilege vs Owner).

AZ-400|T4|Q41.rationale: Black Duck detects open-source libraries and their licensing compliance in the pipeline.

AZ-400|T4|Q44.rationale: Black Duck checks open-source libraries for licensing compliance.

AZ-400|T4|Q46.rationale: App Center iOS distribution requires a .p12 certificate file.

AZ-400|T4|Q50.rationale: WhiteSource Bolt detects open-source libraries added to the codebase for license compliance.

AZ-400|T4|Q51.rationale: The Azure Artifacts Credential Provider automates authenticated NuGet restore.

AZ-400|T4|Q52.rationale: Turn on 'Third-party application access via OAuth' so the Teams app can integrate.

AZ-400|T4|Q53.rationale: Use the GitHub App so pipelines run under the Azure Pipelines identity.

AZ-400|T4|Q56.rationale: Store secrets in (secret) environment variables mapped from pipeline variables so they aren't echoed in logs. NOTE: phrasing is awkward; the intent is to map secret vars to env vars for the task, not log them.

AZ-400|T4|Q59.rationale: PIM requires Azure AD Premium P2; first upgrade the tenant license, then configure time-bound, approval-based activation.

AZ-400|T4|Q60.rationale: GitHub secrets cap at 64 KB, so encrypt the 256-KB secret, store the ciphertext in the repo, and keep the decryption key in a repository-level secret.

AZ-400|T4|Q61.rationale: Push protection for secret scanning requires a GitHub Advanced Security license first.

AZ-400|T4|Q63.rationale: Configure Conditional Access in Azure AD to restrict access to on-premises (trusted-network) devices.

AZ-400|T4|Q64.rationale: A user-assigned managed identity gives multiple VMs one auto-managed identity that can be granted privileges.

AZ-400|T4|Q66.rationale: Recreate the secret at the repository level so all workflows can use it (environment-level scopes to one environment).

AZ-400|T4|Q68.rationale: A user-assigned managed identity shared by the three servers minimizes permission grants (one grant) with least privilege.

AZ-400|T4|Q69.rationale: Microsoft Defender for Containers scans images for vulnerabilities before deployment to Kubernetes.

AZ-400|T4|Q71.rationale: Mend Bolt (formerly WhiteSource Bolt) finds security vulnerabilities in open-source dependencies.

AZ-400|T4|Q72.rationale: Dependabot scans when the dependency graph changes (A) and when a new security advisory is added (E).

AZ-400|T4|Q76.rationale: GitHub's encrypted-secrets REST API requires sealing secrets with libsodium.

AZ-400|T4|Q78.rationale: Add a branch policy status check on the main branch so the custom security validation must pass before deployment.

AZ-400|T5|Q1.rationale: SonarCloud provides continuous code inspection for known problematic patterns.

AZ-400|T5|Q2.rationale: Yes. Reducing code coupling and dependency cycles reduces technical debt.

AZ-400|T5|Q16.rationale: No. Increasing code duplication increases technical debt.

AZ-400|T5|Q42.rationale: CodeQL actions scan all repository code for vulnerabilities.

AZ-400|T5|Q55.rationale: Create a main-branch protection rule (D) requiring review and a CODEOWNERS file (E) assigning the security team as required reviewers.

AZ-400|T6|Q1.rationale: Teams own Epics and Features autonomously while contributing to shared higher-level goals.

AZ-400|T6|Q2.rationale: Kanban boards visualize the flow of work in an agile methodology.

AZ-400|T6|Q4.rationale: A single long-running (main) branch with short-lived feature branches supports parallel work, releasable main, abandonable features and experimentation.

AZ-400|T6|Q32.rationale: Cycle time measures from work start to closed (lead time is creation to closed).

AZ-400|T7|Q2.rationale: Use 'extends: template: template1.yml' so the template wraps and runs before the user steps.

AZ-400|T7|Q10.rationale: No. A hosted Ubuntu agent cannot reach the on-premises dependency system.

AZ-400|T7|Q31.rationale: No. A three-way merge retains the full commit history.

AZ-400|T7|Q37.rationale: Scan images in the CI pipeline (earliest) plus a scheduled scan of the image registry.

AZ-400|T8|Q58.rationale: No. Pipeline artifacts pass files between stages; they do not eliminate the npm install time.

AZ-400|T8|Q59.rationale: Jekyll (B) and DocFX (C) compile Markdown into a static documentation website.

AZ-400|T8|Q60.rationale: A service principal sign-in needs the application (client) ID, a client secret, and the tenant ID.

AZ-400|T8|Q63.rationale: DSC structure is Configuration > Node > Resource.

AZ-400|T8|Q64.rationale: Install the Azure Pipelines app for Teams and subscribe to failure notifications (minimal effort).

AZ-400|T8|Q68.rationale: Azure Traffic Manager gradually shifts a growing subset of users to environment2 (weighted routing).

AZ-400|T8|Q70.rationale: In CalVer, an optional tag like 'beta' goes in the modifier segment.

AZ-400|T8|Q72.rationale: Create a task group so the three tasks can be reused across new pipelines. NOTE: for YAML pipelines a template is preferred; community voted C - verify per pipeline type.

AZ-400|T8|Q74.rationale: Defect escape rate compares defects found in development vs those that escape to released code.

AZ-400|T8|Q75.rationale: Pester is the PowerShell test framework for unit/integration testing DSC configurations.

AZ-400|T8|Q76.rationale: A branch policy can require all PR comments to be resolved before completion (minimal effort).

AZ-400|T9|Q1.rationale: Use a smaller base image on the FROM line to minimize image size. NOTE: depends on the exhibit; verify the FROM line number.

AZ-400|T9|Q2.rationale: Use container jobs to isolate the two pipelines' conflicting dependencies on one agent (minimal cost).

AZ-400|T9|Q3.rationale: Use a Web activity in ADF to retrieve the credential from Key Vault (Vault1).

AZ-400|T9|Q4.rationale: No (per this test series' key); the shown YAML misses one of the fail/publish/always-run requirements.

AI-103|T1|Q10.rationale: Set the project property to the project ID of the Foundry project so the SDK/agent connects to the correct project.

AI-103|T1|Q33.rationale: Application Insights (B) provides the telemetry platform and OpenTelemetry (C) provides the instrumentation SDK to collect and export observability data.

AI-103|T1|Q52.rationale: Configure an Azure Content Understanding in Foundry Tools analyzer first to define how the multi-format documents (tables, logos, varied layouts) should be processed.

AI-103|T1|Q54.rationale: Use advanced data parsing to reingest the documents so the indexer extracts richer, layout-aware content (handles images/tables in PDFs better).

AI-103|T1|Q55.rationale: Use outputFieldMappings to write the OCR-extracted image data into a searchable field in the index.

AI-103|T1|Q57.rationale: Provide labeled samples so the analyzer learns the expected field positions/formats for the varied invoice layouts.

AI-103|T1|Q61.rationale: The indexing pipeline needs Azure OpenAI Embedding (A) for vectorization, Text Split (C) for chunking, and Merge (D) to recombine the pieces. Entity Recognition and Language Detection are not required for this RAG indexing use case.

AI-300|T3|Q5.rationale: For monitoring a deployed online endpoint, the performance metrics to track are Requests per minute (B) and Request latency (E). Feature count and dataset size are training-time concerns.

AI-300|T3|Q8.rationale: To assess responsible AI before deployment: evaluate feature importance for prediction transparency (D) and analyze error rates across defined demographic cohorts (E) for fairness. Latency and schema compatibility are operational, not responsible-AI, checks.

AI-300|T3|Q11.rationale: To register a trained model: first locate and capture the model artifacts from the training run outputs (A), then register the model in the workspace (B). Tagging experiments and exporting locally are not the registration steps.
